skill.name: docx
skill.description: Comprehensive document creation, editing, and analysis with support for tracked changes, comments, formatting preservation, and text extraction. When Claude needs to work with professional documents (.docx files) for: (1) Creating new documents, (2) Modifying or editing content, (3) Working with tracked changes, (4) Adding comments, or any other document tasks
skill.summary: Create, edit, and analyze Microsoft Word documents with tracked changes and comments
skill.author: ZhanlinCui

security_audit.summary: This is a legitimate document manipulation skill for creating and editing Microsoft Word (.docx) files. Static scanner flagged external commands (pandoc, Python scripts), network URLs (XML namespace references in schema files), and filesystem operations (document read/write) - all are expected functionality for this skill. The cryptographic algorithm references in XSD schemas are standard XML schema definitions, not actual implementations. No malicious intent detected.

content.value_statement: This skill enables Claude to create, edit, and analyze Microsoft Word documents with full support for tracked changes, comments, and complex formatting. It provides workflows for document manipulation using Python and JavaScript libraries.
actual_capabilities: Create new Word documents using docx-js JavaScript library
Edit existing Word documents with tracked changes and comments
Extract text and analyze document structure via pandoc
Unpack and modify raw OOXML (Office Open XML) content
Perform document validation and redlining workflows
Handle complex formatting including tables, images, and styles
limitations: Requires pandoc and Python to be installed on the system
Cannot process encrypted or password-protected documents
Complex formatting may require manual intervention
Some Word features not fully supported by underlying libraries

use_cases.title: Generate professional business documents
use_cases.description: Create contracts, reports, and business letters with proper formatting, headers, footers, and styles
use_cases.target_user: Business professionals needing automated document generation

use_cases.title: Review and edit documents with tracked changes
use_cases.description: Review incoming documents, add comments, and make edits with full tracked changes for legal or editorial review

use_cases.title: Batch process document transformations
use_cases.description: Convert multiple documents to markdown, extract content, or apply consistent formatting across document sets

prompt_templates.title: Create a new Word document
prompt_templates.prompt: Create using the docx skill. a new Word document Include a title heading, 3 paragraphs of body text with bold and italic formatting, and a simple table with 3 rows.

prompt_templates.title: Edit existing document with tracked changes
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the redlining workflow to edit document.docx. Replace all instances of 'company' with 'Acme Corp' and add a comment explaining the change. Use tracked changes so the client can review.

prompt_templates.title: Extract text content
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract all text content from report.docx and convert it to markdown format using pandoc with --track-changes=all to preserve any edits.

prompt_templates.title: Analyze document structure
prompt_templates.prompt: Unpack the document to access raw XML. Identify all comments, track changes (insertions and deletions), and embedded images. Provide a summary of the document structure.

output_examples.input: Create a simple welcome letter document
output_examples.output: A Word document with title 'Welcome Letter', formatted body text, and proper business letter styling

output_examples.input: Edit contract.docx to update payment terms from Net 30 to Net 60 with tracked changes enabled
output_examples.output: The document now shows 'Net 60' in tracked changes (deletion of 'Net 30', insertion of 'Net 60'), ready for client review

best_practices: Always read the full documentation files (docx-js.md and ooxml.md) before starting complex document work
Use the redlining workflow for editing documents you do not own to maintain audit trail
Test changes in batches when making multiple edits to ensure each change works correctly
anti_patterns: Avoid skipping the documentation reading step for complex document operations
Do not edit third-party documents without using tracked changes for legal or business documents
Avoid making all changes at once - test incrementally in batches

faq.question: What tools does this skill require?
faq.answer: This skill requires pandoc for text extraction and conversion, and Python with the python-docx library for document manipulation. Node.js is also needed for the docx-js library when creating new documents.

faq.question: Can I edit password-protected documents?
faq.answer: No, this skill cannot edit encrypted or password-protected Word documents. You will need to remove the password protection first.

faq.question: What is the redlining workflow?
faq.answer: The redlining workflow allows you to make tracked changes to documents. It is the recommended approach for editing documents you did not create, especially for legal, academic, or business documents where an audit trail is important.

faq.question: How do I add comments to a document?
faq.answer: Use the Document library in ooxml.md to access the comments functionality. You will need to unpack the document, create a Python script to add comments, and then repack it.

faq.question: Can this skill handle images and tables?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill can handle embedded images and tables. For complex layouts, you may need to work with the raw OOXML directly by unpacking the document.

faq.question: What file formats are supported?
faq.answer: This skill primarily works with .docx files (Office Open XML format). Pandoc can also convert to/from other formats like .doc, .rtf, .odt, and markdown.
